instruction:
To convert a temperature measured in degrees Fahrenheit to degrees Celsius, there is a formula:
${C={\frac{5}{9}\!\left(F-32\right)}}$ where $C$ represents the temperature in degrees Celsius and $F$ represents the temperature in degrees Fahrenheit. If a temperature is $95 {^\circ}\text{F}$, what is that temperature measured in Celsius? Use degC for $^{\circ}\text{C}$ and degF for $^{\circ}\text{F}$. [ANS]
